fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arkansas Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arkansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity arkansas, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to arkansas, respiratory therapist compact arkansas, arkansas respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Arkansas State Medical Board — RT
board.mailing_address: Arkansas State Medical Board, 1401 West Capitol Avenue, Suite 340, Little Rock, AR 72201-2936

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Annually, on the last day of the licensee's birth month
quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Arkansas.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Register for an account on the Arkansas State Medical Board Online Application System and complete the Respiratory Care Practitioner Initial Application online.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your CoARC-approved respiratory care program to be sent directly from your school to the Arkansas State Medical Board.

steps.title: Submit Official NBRC Scores
steps.description: Request official score reports for both your Certified Respiratory Therapist (CRT) and Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT) exams to be sent directly from the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) to the Board.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Checks
steps.description: Complete a state and federal criminal background check, which requires fingerprinting by the Arkansas State Police and FBI. Also, complete a Child Maltreatment Registry check through the Department of Human Services (DHS).

steps.title: Pay Application and License Fees
steps.description: Pay the required initial application fee and, upon approval, the initial license fee through the Arkansas State Medical Board Online Application System.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Arkansas respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Arkansas have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Arkansas. To work in Arkansas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Arkansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arkansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Arkansas
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license in Arkansas, you must create an account and complete the online application through the Arkansas State Medical Board Online Application System.

official_links.portal_name: Arkansas State Medical Board Online Application System
official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the portal URL, click 'Create an Account' and enter your first name, last name, and email address to register. Once registered, log in and select 'Respiratory Care Practitioner Initial Application' or 'Respiratory Care Practitioner Endorsement Application' to start your application.
